chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI. C.P. No. D-2298 of 2021 ____________________________________________________________ Dated Order with signature of Judge. ____________________________________________________________ For hearing of main case 29.01.2026. Mr. Muhammad Siddique Memon, Advocate for Petitioner. Mr. Abdul Jalil, A.A.G. --------------- The Petitioner namely Mst. Farhat Qazi has filed the instant Petition under Article 199 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, seeking the following relief: a) Call detail report from the respondents with regards the remaining death/retirement benefits of the deceased. b) To direct the respondents to immediately settle the account of the deceased Abdul Mannan Siddiqui and issue cheques of remaining benefits of the deceased to his widow/ petitioner. c) To hold enquiry as to why the respondents failed to hand over the death benefits of the deceased after passing of three years. d) That this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to allow this petition with compensatory cost, as the petitioner sustained mental torture and agony due to the act of the respondents, so also she suffered financial hardship. e) Any other relief which this Hon'ble Court deem fit and proper under the circumstances of the case. f) Cost of the Petition may be awarded. Learned counsel for the Petitioner submitted that her husband, Abdul Manan Siddiqui, was serving as Stenographer (BPS-16) in D.M.C. East since 1996 and passed away on 14.02.2018. The pension commutation amount was not released, and despite repeated requests for Group Insurance, the Respondents took no action, leading to the filing of the instant Petition.

East since 1996 and passed away on 14.02.2018. The pension commutation amount was not released, and despite repeated requests for Group Insurance, the Respondents took no action, leading to the filing of the instant Petition. Reference was made to the Hon’ble Supreme Court’s decision in Haji Muhammad Ismail Memon (PLD 2007 SC 35), directing the Chief Secretary, Sindh, to finalize pensionary benefits, which the Respondents failed to implement. Today, learned counsel informed that pursuant to the Order dated 21.01.2026, Rs. 9,00,000/- has been disbursed to the Petitioner’s account, leaving no further grievance. However, clarification is needed regarding the accounts under which the two cheques were issued. The petition is disposed of accordingly. The Respondents shall provide the required account details to the petitioner within one week. JUDGE JUDGE Jamil
